questions.q: What are the two types of averages?
questions.answer: The two types of averages are arithmetic mean and weighted average. Arithmetic mean is the sum of all values divided by the number of values, whereas weighted average is the sum of the product of each value and its respective weight divided by the sum of the weights.

questions.q: What is dispersion and why is it necessary?
questions.answer: Dispersion refers to the scattering or spread of data values from the central value. It is necessary to understand the dispersion because it helps in identifying the variability in data values and in making decisions based on the data.

questions.q: What is the difference between range and interquartile range?
questions.answer: Range is the difference between the highest and lowest values in a data set, whereas interquartile range (IQR) is the difference between the third quartile (Q3) and the first quartile (Q1). IQR is a better measure of dispersion than range because it is less affected by extreme values.

questions.q: What is the purpose of graphical representation of data?
questions.answer: The purpose of graphical representation of data is to enable the reader to understand the data quickly and easily, and to identify trends and patterns in the data.

questions.q: What are the advantages of using histograms?
questions.answer: The advantages of using histograms are that they are easy to understand and provide a visual representation of the frequency distribution of data. They are also useful in identifying the shape of the distribution and in comparing the distribution of different data sets.

questions.q: What is the difference between a bar chart and a pie chart?
questions.answer: A bar chart is used to compare the values of different categories, whereas a pie chart is used to show the proportion of different categories in a data set.

questions.q: What are the uses of frequency polygons?
questions.answer: The uses of frequency polygons are that they provide a visual representation of the frequency distribution of data and are useful in comparing the distribution of different data sets.

questions.q: What is the purpose of using a scatter diagram?
questions.answer: The purpose of using a scatter diagram is to identify the relationship between two variables and to understand the direction and strength of the relationship.

questions.q: What are the limitations of box and whisker plot?
questions.answer: The limitations of box and whisker plot are that it is difficult to read and interpret, and it is not suitable for large data sets. It is also not useful in identifying the shape of the distribution.

questions.q: What is the difference between a percentage bar diagram and a simple bar diagram?
questions.answer: A percentage bar diagram is used to show the percentage distribution of different categories, whereas a simple bar diagram is used to compare the values of different categories.
